skill.name: shareone
skill.description: Publish HTML, Markdown, text, documents, presentations, images, and archives to ShareOne, then manage public share links, downloads, settings, and comments.
skill.summary: Share local files and generated content through ShareOne public links.
skill.author: sudoprivacy

security_audit.summary: Static external-command and weak-crypto alerts are mostly false positives: reviewed lines are Markdown command examples, JavaScript template strings, URL parsing, timeout handling, and crypto.randomBytes for multipart boundaries. The skill still has confirmed high-risk behavior because it reads local files, sends content and API keys over the network, stores credentials locally, prints API keys, and can derive an authenticated request host from a user-supplied URL.

high_findings.title: API Key Can Be Sent to a User-Supplied Host
high_findings.description: update_share_settings.js derives SHAREONE_BASE_URL from a full URL supplied as the target link, then sends authenticated PUT requests through shareone_client.js. A non-ShareOne URL could receive the X-API-Key header or Sudowork proxy authorization flow, creating a credential disclosure risk.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The host is taken from user-controlled URL input and later used by the authenticated request builder. The reviewed code shows no allowlist that restricts this flow to shareone.app.

high_findings.title: API Keys Are Printed to Command Output
high_findings.description: The credential check and guest-key scripts print API keys in machine-readable output. The workflows then instruct the assistant to display newly created keys and binding links to the user, which can persist secrets in transcripts and logs.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct code evidence shows API key values are written to stdout. The workflow also explicitly requires user-facing display of generated key material.

medium_findings.title: Local Files Are Uploaded to External Services
medium_findings.description: The skill reads user-selected local files and uploads them to ShareOne, Azure, or S3 upload URLs returned by ShareOne. This is core functionality, but it creates data exposure risk if sensitive files are selected or consent is mishandled.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file reads and outbound upload requests are explicit. The workflow includes a publish consent step, so this is high-impact legitimate functionality rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: API Keys Are Stored Locally Without Permission Hardening
medium_findings.description: Local ShareOne API keys are written to a home-directory credentials file as JSON. The code does not set restrictive file permissions or encrypt the credential before writing it.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code writes the raw API key value directly to disk and no chmod or encryption step appears near the write. Local config storage can be legitimate, but this implementation has weak protection.

medium_findings.title: Owner Downloads Can Bypass Public Download Restrictions
medium_findings.description: The download workflow first attempts owner download when credentials are present, which bypasses public password and allow-download restrictions for owned links. This is documented behavior but should be clear to users because it changes privacy expectations.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The behavior is explicit in code and documentation. It appears intended for owner workflows, so the concern is user expectation and access control clarity rather than malicious intent.

low_findings.title: External Command Alerts Are Mostly False Positives
low_findings.description: Most static external-command matches are Markdown code examples or JavaScript template literals. I found no evidence in the reviewed files of child_process, eval, shell execution, or backticks executing OS commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Reviewed representative locations contain command documentation or string interpolation. No shell execution API was found in the inspected implementation files.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto Alerts Are False Positives in Reviewed Code
low_findings.description: The reviewed weak-crypto locations do not show MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or custom encryption. The only crypto use found is crypto.randomBytes for a multipart boundary, which is appropriate for that purpose.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted review found URL parsing, timeout handling, and multipart boundary generation rather than weak cryptographic algorithms. No evidence found for insecure hashing or encryption in the reviewed code.

low_findings.title: No Prompt Injection Pattern Found
low_findings.description: I searched the skill instructions, workflows, and scripts for explicit override, ignore, pre-approved, and skip-security language from the audit warning list. No evidence found in the searched files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The search covered the listed suspicious phrases across the provided skill files. This does not prove absence of every possible injection, but no direct warning-pattern evidence was found.

dangerous_patterns.title: User-Controlled Base URL for Authenticated Requests
dangerous_patterns.description: Authenticated update requests can use a base URL derived from user input, which is dangerous when API keys are attached automatically.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The relevant assignments and header attachment are explicit. There is no visible host allowlist in the reviewed path.

dangerous_patterns.title: Secret Values in Standard Output
dangerous_patterns.description: API key values are emitted in status strings such as KEY_FOUND and GUEST_KEY_CREATED.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The output strings directly include the API key variable. This is a confirmed secret exposure pattern.

dangerous_patterns.title: Filesystem Read Followed by Network Upload
dangerous_patterns.description: The upload scripts read local file contents and transmit them through HTTP requests to ShareOne or returned cloud upload URLs.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code directly combines local file reads with outbound network requests. It is intended functionality, but still a dangerous pattern for marketplace review.

content.value_statement: Sharing generated files often requires manual uploads and link setup. This skill automates ShareOne publishing, updates, downloads, and comment workflows from an AI coding session.
actual_capabilities: Publishes HTML, Markdown, and text files as ShareOne pages.
Uploads PDFs, Office documents, images, archives, and presentations as binary files.
Creates or stores ShareOne API keys for local and Sudowork environments.
Updates existing ShareOne links with passwords, watermarks, custom slugs, and comment settings.
Downloads ShareOne link content when owner credentials or public download permissions allow it.
Guides comment viewing, processing, and republishing workflows.
limitations: Requires ShareOne network access and a valid API key for authenticated operations.
Published content is sent to an external hosting service and may become publicly reachable.
Credential handling uses local files, environment variables, command arguments, and command output.
The skill is specific to ShareOne workflows and does not provide general cloud storage support.

use_cases.title: Share a Generated Page
use_cases.description: Publish an HTML or Markdown page from an AI session and return a ShareOne link.

use_cases.title: Distribute Review Files
use_cases.description: Upload a presentation, PDF, or design file with optional password and watermark controls.

use_cases.title: Process Link Feedback
use_cases.description: Download shared content, review comments, apply changes, and update the existing ShareOne link.

prompt_templates.title: Publish a File
prompt_templates.prompt: Publish this local HTML file to ShareOne and give me the share link: <file path>.

prompt_templates.title: Publish With Protection
prompt_templates.prompt: Upload this PDF to ShareOne with the password <password> and watermark <watermark text>.

prompt_templates.title: Update Link Settings
prompt_templates.scenario: Change metadata on an existing share
prompt_templates.prompt: Update this ShareOne link with a new custom slug and enable comments: <ShareOne link>.

prompt_templates.title: Review and Republish
prompt_templates.prompt: Fetch comments from this ShareOne page, revise the source page based on them, republish it, and summarize the changes.

output_examples.input: Publish my generated project overview page to ShareOne.
output_examples.output: The assistant checks credentials, asks for publish consent, uploads the page, and returns a ShareOne link with optional feature reminders.

output_examples.input: Download this ShareOne file and summarize it.
output_examples.output: The assistant downloads the available file, saves it locally, identifies the content type, and summarizes the accessible content.

output_examples.input: Add a password and watermark to this existing ShareOne link.
output_examples.output: The assistant updates the link settings without re-uploading the source file and reports the resulting status.

best_practices: Review content before publishing because ShareOne links can expose private or sensitive information.
Use passwords and watermarks for files that need limited sharing.
Store API keys in the intended secret store instead of passing them repeatedly in prompts.
anti_patterns: Do not publish secrets, customer data, private source code, or regulated content without explicit review.
Do not use arbitrary non-ShareOne URLs for authenticated update operations.
Do not paste long-lived API keys into shared chats or logs.

faq.question: What does this skill publish?
faq.answer: It publishes text pages, HTML, Markdown, PDFs, Office files, images, archives, and presentations to ShareOne.

faq.question: Does it require an API key?
faq.answer: Authenticated publishing, updating, and owner downloads require a ShareOne API key or a managed Sudowork secret.

faq.question: Can it update an existing link?
faq.answer: Yes. It can update text page content and change settings such as password, watermark, slug, and comments.

faq.question: Can it download any ShareOne link?
faq.answer: No. Downloads require owner credentials or public download permission, and password-protected links need the password.

faq.question: Is published content private by default?
faq.answer: No. The skill creates public share links, so users should review content before publishing.

faq.question: Why is this skill high risk?
faq.answer: It handles API keys, reads local files, sends content to external services, and has unsafe credential exposure patterns.
